channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires: 16 dead, more than a dozen missing as crews continue fighting blazes
item.description: More than a dozen people have died as a result of the still-spreading wildfires burning in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KRON4

item.title: Steve Bannon says he will ‘take down’ the ‘truly evil’ Elon Musk
item.description: “I will have Elon Musk run out of here by Inauguration Day,” Bannon said, adding “He is a truly evil guy, a very bad guy. I made it my personal thing to take this guy down.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Canada deploying 60 firefighters to help with California blazes
item.description: In a post on X, Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Canada was helping aid the Los Angeles area as several wildfires continue devastating the area.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump: Jack Smith shouldn't be allowed to release report
item.description: "Why would Deranged Jack Smith be allowed to issue a “report” on a complete and total Witch Hunt against me, strictly for political purposes,” Trump said in a Truth Social post.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Rahm Emanuel steps down as Japan ambassador ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: “Last year, I spoke at the U.S. Naval War College and found it both rewarding and reaffirming,” Emanuel said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Biden and Netanyahu discuss the latest in the effort to reach an Israel-Hamas war ceasefire
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and U.S. President Joe Biden spoke Sunday about efforts to reach a ceasefire and hostage release deal in the Israel-Hamas war, a sign of the intensifying push to reach a deal before Donald Trump’s inauguration next week.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: EU ministers will consider easing sanctions on Syria at a meeting later in January
item.description: RIYADH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — European Union foreign ministers will meet in late January to discuss easing sanctions imposed on Syria, the bloc's foreign policy chief said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: BARAA ANWER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Woman dead, 2 people injured after 3 stabbings at 16th Street Mall
item.description: Denver police are looking for a single suspect who may have committed three stabbings Saturday night, leaving a woman dead and two men injured at the 16th Street Mall between 5 p.m. and 6 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about the devastation from the Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fires ripping through the Los Angeles area have killed at least 16 people, displaced thousands of others and destroyed more than 12,000 structures while burning through an area larger than the city of San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: A dad and daughter help evacuees learn whether their homes survived the California wildfires
item.description: As the fire roared down a hillside toward their Altadena home, Vanessa Prata and her parents hurried to pack their car. They focused on saving irreplaceable items, like family photographs and a baby doll from Vanessa's childhood. But they didn't leave.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Vance says those who ‘committed violence’ on Jan. 6 ‘obviously’ shouldn’t be pardoned
item.description: Vice President-elect JD Vance said people who “committed violence” on Jan. 6 “obviously” shouldn’t be pardoned by President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden speaks with relatives of Americans held by Taliban, but deal to bring them home still elusive
item.description: President Joe Biden has spoken with relatives of three Americans the U.S. government is looking to bring home from Afghanistan, but no agreement has been reached on a deal to get them back.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Reality TV star Chase Chrisley accused of slapping Twin Peaks bar manager in Atlanta
item.description: Chrisley, who gained fame on the “Chrisley Knows Best” show that featured his parents and close-knit family, was allegedly drunk at a Twin Peaks restaurant on Jan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fencing, roof of Anchorage pedestrian bridge fall in strong wind, closing highway
item.description: The side fencing and roof of a pedestrian bridge that fell in strong wind closed the main highway south of Anchorage, Alaska, early Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Reality TV star Chase Chrisley accused of slapping a Twin Peaks bar manager in Atlanta
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Reality TV star Chase Chrisley is accused of slapping the manager of a popular sports bar in Atlanta after refusing to leave.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Vance says Jan. 6 participants who committed violence 'obviously' shouldn't be pardoned
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President-elect JD Vance says people responsible for the violence during the Capitol riot “obviously” should not be pardoned, as President-elect Donald Trump is promising to use his clemency power on behalf of many of those who tried on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: New England Patriots hire former Super Bowl champ Mike Vrabel as next coach
item.description: Vrabel, a fan favorite during eight seasons as a player in New England where he was a member of its first three Super Bowl winners and an inductee into the team’s Hall of Fame in 2023, was the preferred candidate because of his long association with the franchise and coaching success.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: PHOTOS: West Virginia father-son duo build ice dragon igloo
item.description: While many of us were staying cozy indoors, a father-son duo was hard at work making the best of this week's wintery weather by building a one-of-a-kind igloo.
item.dc:creator: Sam Gorski, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires: 16 dead, more than a dozen missing as crews continue fighting blazes
item.description: More than a dozen people have died as a result of the still-spreading wildfires burning in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, WGN 9

item.title: Man says two of his cats died after drinking raw milk recalled for bird flu
item.description: “It’s horrible when you realize that you’re the one that actually gave them the milk that killed them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Los Angeles tree advocates hope their vital work recovers from devastating fires
item.description: Some people in Los Angeles have worked for years to increase the number of trees that provide shade and respite from heat and air pollution.
item.dc:creator: Tammy Webber And Dorany Pineda, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: IRS delays after ID thefts 'unconscionable,' watchdog says. Here are 3 ways to protect yourself
item.description: A new tax season is nearly here, and with it an opportunity for identity thieves to take advantage of taxpayers.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California man says two of his cats died after drinking raw milk recalled for bird flu
item.description: A California man whose two cats died after drinking raw milk recalled for bird flu risk says he meant to keep his beloved pets healthy, but his efforts tragically backfired.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's talk of expansion puts world leaders on alert
item.description: President-elect Trump’s talk of territorial expansion has rattled world leaders at an already precarious time in global politics. Trump doubled down last week on his suggestions that the U.S. buy Greenland, take control of the Panama Canal and make Canada “the 51st state.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires death toll rises as crews fight heavy winds to save homes and landmarks
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The death toll from the wildfires ravaging the Los Angeles area rose to 16 as crews battled to cut off the spreading blazes before potentially strong winds return that could push the flames toward some of the city’s most famous landmarks.
item.dc:creator: JAE HONG, HOLLY RAMER and MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Biden gives Pope Francis highest civilian honor before leaving office
item.description: Biden, who is only the second Catholic president in U.S. history, spoke with the Pope by phone on Saturday to tell him he was a recipient of the medal, which is the highest civilian honor.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Walmart recalls 12,000 cartons of chicken broth
item.description: Walmart has recalled over 12,000 cartons of Great Value Family Size Chicken Broth due to packaging issues that could lead to spoilage, according to the Food and Drug Administration (FDA).
item.dc:creator: Reagan Wynn, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gun jams as woman tries to shoot boyfriend: Tennessee police
item.description: A Tennessee woman is facing an attempted murder charge after reportedly attempting to shoot her boyfriend before her gun jammed, Memphis Police said.
item.dc:creator: Raven Moore, WGN 9

item.title: MAP: Emergency room visits for respiratory illnesses are soaring in these states
item.description: A number of states are seeing a spike in hospital visits caused by respiratory illnesses as we enter the New Year.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, ABC 4

item.title: Are there no Michelin-starred restaurants near you? There's a reason for that
item.description: You'd have difficulty finding Michelin-starred restaurants in most states — but it's not because there aren't excellent eaters in those places.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: How a week of weather extremes upended the lives of millions of Americans
item.description: It was a week of fire and ice. It began with millions of people across the U.S. shivering amid blizzard conditions and frigid air that lasted for days, thanks to a jet stream that slips out of its usual path more often these days.
item.dc:creator: MELINA WALLING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Power grid faults surged right before Los Angeles wildfires began: expert
item.description: A company that monitors electrical activity says faults along the Los Angeles power grid soared in the same areas where three of this week’s major wildfires are occurring.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Excerpts from President-elect Donald Trump’s sentencing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump spoke for six minutes. The judge talked for seven. In the end, the first criminal court sentencing of a former or future president took little more than half an hour.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: LIVE: Fires devastating LA grow more slowly as winds die down
item.description: The two biggest fires devastating the Los Angeles area grew just slightly Friday as firefighters beat back the blazes that have killed at least 10 people, obliterated neighborhoods and left the nation's second-largest city on edge.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KRON4

item.title: Wildfires latest: 153,000 residents still under evacuation as Kenneth Fire order lifted
item.description: Firefighters are hoping for a break Friday from the fierce winds that have fueled massive blazes in the Los Angeles area, killing 10 people, obliterating whole neighborhoods and setting the nation’s second-largest city on edge.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: LIVE: Fires devastating LA grow more slowly as winds die down
item.description: The two biggest fires devastating the Los Angeles area grew just slightly Friday as firefighters beat back the blazes that have killed at least 10 people, obliterated neighborhoods and left the nation's second-largest city on edge.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: The Los Angeles landmarks from film, TV damaged by wildfires
item.description: The high school where Brian De Palma brought Stephen King’s “Carrie” to life, Will Rogers’ ranch house, and a motel owned by William Randolph Hearst are among some of the famous structures that have been damaged or destroyed by the California wildfires.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
